title: Vigilance Is Needed; Victory Is Ours!
translatorNote: This translation is intended to carry Kalaignar's voice into clear, contemporary English rather than recast the letter as literary or academic prose. It preserves the polling-eve urgency, extended political rebuttal, repeated rhetorical questions, project names, dates, figures, attributed allegations, and paragraph order. The Tamil below is a source-checked derived reading copy in which clear OCR character, spacing, malformed Unicode, and word-break errors are repaired. Source-printed political phrasing, including the deliberate rendering `கெலிகாப்டர்`, is retained. The canonical source remains unchanged and authoritative. `Udanpirappē` is retained in Tamil transliteration rather than flattened into “brother,” “sister,” or “comrade.” Literally evoking “one born alongside me,” Kalaignar uses it as a distinctive address of shared identity, equality, affection, and solidarity within the movement.
paragraphs: Tomorrow, polling for the parliamentary general election will take place in Tamil Nadu. For the past two or three months, you have watered the crop every day, guarded it without blinking, removed the weeds, applied manure and raised it. Tomorrow is the day you will see the harvest of your labour! Campaigning over loudspeakers ended yesterday evening. On behalf of our movement, the General Secretary, the Professor, and I disregarded age and physical weakness and travelled as much as we could by car and van to campaign. The newspapers themselves noted that, among political leaders across India, the Professor, Kerala's Achuthanandan and I were the three who campaigned in this election after crossing the age of ninety.

There is no need even to speak of our Treasurer, younger brother Stalin. He travelled by van and campaigned throughout Tamil Nadu without leaving out a single constituency. There is scarcely a newspaper that did not praise his campaign. You have personally seen and heard how, at every meeting, he explained in language the gathered people could easily understand both the DMK government's work and achievements for Tamil Nadu and the administrative disorder and suffering under the AIADMK government. He gave firm, evidence-based answers to Chief Minister Jayalalithaa's questions whenever they arose.

The movement's front-line leaders, members of the arts world, party speakers and members of its various wings also campaigned. They included Deputy General Secretaries sister Sarguna Pandian and V.P. Duraisamy, and Kanimozhi, Rahman Khan, Tiruchi Siva, Bishop Ezra Sargunam, Vagai Chandrasekar, Khushbu Sundar, Cumbum Selvendran, Professor Sabapathy Mohan, Noorjahan Begum, Sankari Narayanan, Pulavar Indira Kumari, Pudukkottai Vijaya, Thanjai Kootharasan, V.P. Rajan, Nellikuppam Pugazhenthi, Dindigul Leoni, Kumarimuthu, Vasu Vikram, Inigo Irudayaraj and Manushya Puthiran, together with leading figures from our allied parties. Most importantly, Dravidar Kazhagam President, Tamilar Thalaivar Veeramani, campaigned every single day and explained scientifically why people must vote for the DMK alliance. Deputy General Secretary Duraimurugan accompanied me on my tour. As leader of the movement, I thank them all on behalf of the Democratic Progressive Alliance.

Who campaigned against us for the AIADMK? Its General Secretary and Chief Minister, Jayalalithaa, alone! Without being jolted or shaken, she travelled everywhere by helicopter. People brought to the meetings were held there hungry and thirsty under the cruel sun, unable to leave even to attend to their natural needs. Before them, she read every line of a prepared speech, paused to ask herself, “Will you do it? Will you do it?” and poured out as many falsehoods as she could at every meeting.

Speaking in Chennai yesterday, Chief Minister Jayalalithaa imported one enormous lie: that she had not conducted closing ceremonies for the many schemes begun under the DMK government, and that the AIADMK government would never hesitate to implement a public-welfare scheme. I answered this in detail at yesterday's Anna Nagar meeting. The first claim in her bundle of falsehoods was: “The Maduravoyal-Chennai Port Elevated Expressway was brought by the Union Government. We did not oppose that project completely.” This appeared in her own party newspaper, so she cannot deny it.

But everyone in Tamil Nadu knows who stopped the Maduravoyal-Chennai Port Elevated Expressway; that the contractor implementing it went to court seeking more than Rs 600 crore in compensation; that the Union Government sent senior officials to appeal to the Tamil Nadu Government; and that, despite all this, the Tamil Nadu Government, Jayalalithaa in particular, prevented the project from proceeding merely because the Prime Minister had laid its foundation stone during the DMK administration.

Even after the Madras High Court finally permitted the project to continue, it is worth remembering that the Jayalalithaa government went to the Supreme Court, just as it had done over the Samacheer Kalvi uniform school education scheme. Concealing all this, Jayalalithaa campaigned in Chennai saying that they had not opposed the elevated expressway completely. How the people who heard her must have laughed! They must have thought: who else could lie to this extent like our Chief Minister? No one could compete with her in telling falsehoods!

This is how, when Jayalalithaa began her campaign in March, she uttered the Himalayan falsehood that she herself had granted 3.5 per cent reservation to Muslims. Muslim leaders immediately issued statement after statement saying it had been granted during Kalaignar's government. She then uttered another falsehood, claiming that she had neither supported kar seva nor sent people for it. In reply, I cited the newspaper interview in which the BJP thanked Jayalalithaa for sending people to participate in kar seva. I also cited her speech supporting kar seva at the Chief Ministers' Conference in Delhi.

Thereafter, on the Sri Lankan Tamil issue, the Cauvery, Mullaperiyar and fishermen's problems, and every other issue on which Jayalalithaa played a double role and deceived the people, she turned the facts upside down and continued telling one falsehood after another. I gave detailed explanations each time.

Yesterday Jayalalithaa said the AIADMK government would not hesitate to implement public-welfare schemes and would likewise not hesitate to stop schemes hostile to the people. As soon as she assumed office, did she not shut down the Secretariat and Legislative Assembly constructed and functioning properly in the Omandurar Government Estate under the DMK? Was that a scheme hostile to the people? Is Jayalalithaa ready to answer?
She said she would not implement Samacheer Kalvi. Was that a scheme hostile to the people?

To mark the centenary of Perarignar Anna, we built a library unmatched elsewhere. It functioned splendidly, benefited everyone, earned praise and was used by research scholars. Who announced that this very library would be converted into a hospital? Was it not Jayalalithaa? Even after a court ordered that the library must continue, has it not been left without maintenance? Was that a self-serving scheme? Even today, its employees have issued a statement saying their salaries have not been paid properly.

We realised the century-old Tamil dream of securing Classical Language status for Tamil, obtained the Union Government's approval and brought the Central Institute of Classical Tamil to Tamil Nadu. Was that a self-serving scheme?
During the DMK government, we established and properly operated the Pavendhar Research Library in the Secretariat in Chennai. Was that a self-serving scheme?
How rapidly was the Metro Rail project progressing then, and is it not now moving at a tortoise's pace? Is that not a public-welfare project?

In the heart of Chennai, the DMK government recovered government land from private hands and created Semmozhi Poonga, where hundreds of Chennai residents came every day to enjoy themselves. Was that a self-serving scheme?

Why were infrastructure highway projects worth Rs 15,000 crore, including the Chennai-Tirupati broad-road project, stopped? Were none of them public-welfare projects? Why did Jayalalithaa block them all?

What sin did ordinary, poor People's Welfare Workers commit? For the sole reason that they had been appointed by the DMK government, the Jayalalithaa government has chased and beaten them away. Was that a self-serving scheme? Are those People's Welfare Workers not Tamils? How many died by suicide after being dismissed? Their families now stand abandoned in the street. Was that a self-serving scheme?
We fulfilled the dream of Tamils and scholars by declaring the first day of Thai the Tamil New Year. Was it not reversed under the AIADMK government? Was that a self-serving scheme?
We established welfare boards for unorganised workers, and they functioned well. Was that not a public-welfare programme? Which of these was not for public welfare?
Without the slightest shame, Jayalalithaa said that the Sethusamudram Project, which she claimed would destroy fishermen's livelihoods, was an inexhaustible vessel for Karunanidhi's family!

On 23 July 1967, Perarignar Anna joined allied parties in holding Awakening Day meetings across Tamil Nadu to press the Union Government to implement the Sethusamudram Project. Was that a self-serving scheme?

When some people went to court seeking to stop the project's inaugural ceremony, a Bench headed by the Chief Justice of the Madras High Court ruled: “The petitioner has rushed to court with the object of obstructing the Sethusamudram Project, which has been brought forward in the national interest. Everyone knows that the project will bring substantial benefit to the country. At present, ships must sail around Sri Lanka to reach the Bay of Bengal. A shipping canal across the Palk Strait will save a great deal of money and time. This canal project will produce beneficial results. There can therefore be no stay of the ceremony.” Does Jayalalithaa seek to impute an ulterior motive to that judgment too?

In both the AIADMK's 2001 and 2004 election manifestos, this same Jayalalithaa said that the Sethusamudram Project would benefit not only our country but the nations of Southeast Asia and every country with coastal regions; that trade and industry would grow; and that, recognising the project's need and importance, her party would ceaselessly press the Union Government to give it priority and complete it within a fixed period. Was the Sethusamudram Project then an inexhaustible vessel for Jayalalithaa's family? Does she oppose it now because that revenue is no longer available? Is that the true reason?

Leaders of many Tamil Nadu parties issued statements welcoming the project. Perunthalaivar Kamaraj and our dear friend M.G.R. pressed for it. Does Jayalalithaa say that all of them sought the project for selfish reasons? Did all of them regard Sethusamudram as an inexhaustible vessel for their families?

Jayalalithaa said that she alone cared about the fishermen's problem and no one else did. Prime Minister Dr Manmohan Singh himself said that the Union Government had written to the Tamil Nadu Government asking it to specify a date for representatives of fishermen from the two countries to meet, and that no reply had come from Tamil Nadu. Is that true or not? Was that the concern Jayalalithaa showed for fishermen?

The Prime Minister wrote as early as 16 March 2013. Why was there no reply? Six months later, on 20 September 2013, the Chief Minister finally wrote conveying Tamil Nadu's consent to a meeting between representatives of the two countries' fishermen. In his letter dated 9 November 2013, the Prime Minister himself thanked her for it. If Jayalalithaa had truly cared about the suffering of Tamil Nadu's fishermen, would she have delayed answering the Prime Minister by six months? Whenever fishermen are arrested, does writing one letter to the Prime Minister and hurriedly publicising it in the newspapers amount to protecting Indian fishermen?

Jayalalithaa toured Chennai for two days. What did she see? Since she travelled only along the main roads, she could not have seen certain things. Under the DMK government, Rs 34.72 crore was allocated for a flyover at Porur and construction began. Why was the work stopped after the AIADMK took office?

The DMK government began the Rs 49.55 crore Moolakadai flyover; the Rs 60.23 crore flyover at the junction of Anna Nagar Second Avenue, Thirumangalam-Mogappair Road and Inner Ring Road; the Rs 69.43 crore flyover at the junction of Inner Ring Road and NSK Salai in Vadapalani; the Rs 117 crore flyover and underpass at the junction of Periyar E.V.R. Salai, Nelson Manickam Road and Anna Arch; the Rs 80.68 crore Vyasarpadi railway overbridge; and the Rs 1,081.40 crore Chennai Outer Ring Road. All these works stand frozen under the AIADMK government. Are they public-welfare works, or self-serving works?

Consider Chennai itself. Sewage mixing with drinking water has not been prevented. Instead of removing rubbish daily, 821 garbage collection centres have been created where waste is concealed. They have become major causes of insanitary conditions, mosquito breeding and new diseases. Mosquitoes, potholed roads, streetlights that do not burn, storm-water drains that are not desilted and underground sewers that are not repaired are found everywhere. Does the AIADMK government or the Chennai Corporation care about any of this? Does Jayalalithaa care?

Jayalalithaa pays no attention to the voting public, makes no effort to solve people's problems, and continually encourages anti-democratic conduct both within and outside the Assembly. She has no right to lecture anyone on public interest and self-interest. Having accumulated assets allegedly worth more than Rs 5,000 crore and postponed both time and justice through repeated adjournments, she has no right to preach about corruption. Tamil Nadu's voters understand this very well and are ready to teach this government a sound lesson.

That is why, several days ago, I explained the principle: “One vote from every member; one vote through every member.” Every member of the movement must keep that basic rule in mind, cast at least one vote personally and secure as many additional votes as possible for the Democratic Progressive Alliance's candidates. I ask you to make every effort within your power to ensure that all our candidates win by enormous margins.

Because the opposition parties are eager to do anything whatsoever to win this election, the agents appointed for our alliance's candidates must act with complete attention and the greatest vigilance. They must remain highly alert after polling ends, while the electronic voting machines are taken to the proper locations and while they are secured at the counting centres.
This letter keeps growing longer. The district secretaries and candidates are waiting, saying it is already time for me to leave on the campaign in the van.
Attention, attention! After working briskly for the past several days, we must not be deceived in the final two days.
Vigilance is needed; victory is ours!
With affection,
M.K.
